action.reasoning: Observation: direct goal-compass baseline is valid but fails because it cannot navigate around walls; introspection confirmed pathfinder.find_path works and localization yaw matches world motion, and turn calibration showed positive angular velocity increases compass angle. Analysis: implement a navmesh waypoint heuristic in predict.py/model.py: when far from the humanoid, compute a shortest path from Spot to human and drive toward a lookahead path point using localization yaw; when within social distance, switch to direct distance/facing control. This should improve finding and following without expensive RL. Decision: update the policy and predictor, set checkpoint params to the calibrated turn_sign=-1, run a 2-episode local smoke test, then submit eval.
action.command: python train.py --save_dir checkpoints/baseline && mkdir -p local_eval && python predict.py --data_path "$(pwd)/local_eval" --checkpoint "$(pwd)/checkpoints/baseline" --output "$(pwd)/local_eval/out_planner.json" --num_episodes 2
